rationale: The citing paper uses Iyengar, Ansari, and Gupta (2007) in core positioning of its contribution, not just as background. In the introduction, it groups that paper with prior flat-rate-bias explanations based on tariff-specific fixed effects and then argues this approach is limited for counterfactual tariff design. It also cites Iyengar et al. when contrasting prior assumptions (e.g., negligible switching costs/dynamics) with its own dynamic learning-and-switching-cost framework, and later as a benchmark for switching-cost magnitudes. These functions materially shape framing and interpretation in the citing paper.
relation_description: The citing paper explicitly contrasts its model with Iyengar et al.'s approach, arguing that prior tariff-choice models relying on tariff-specific preference terms and simpler dynamics are limited, and proposing an alternative Bayesian learning plus switching-cost account of flat-rate bias. It also uses Iyengar et al.'s estimated switching costs as a comparison point for interpreting its own estimates.
